events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes an electrical terminations & splices inspection on a residential job. The observed condition is "insufficient torque" detected via thermal signature (IR modality). Work stage is rough-complete. Reference: NEC 110.14.

NEC 110.14 requires electrical connections to be properly torqued. Insufficient torque causes heating at terminals - thermal signature on IR. This is a defect/non-compliance.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, potential fire hazard)
Risk: high (heating at connections can cause fire)
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 85%
Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading given)
Confidence: high, maybe 0.9
Findings: insufficient torque on terminations causing thermal anomaly
Actions: re-torque to manufacturer specs
References: NEC 110.14

  "findings": [
    "Insufficient torque on electrical termination detected via IR thermal signature — connection heating under load",
    "Loose/under-torqued terminal violates NEC 110.14 requirement for proper mechanical and electrical integrity",
    "Thermal anomaly indicates high-resistance joint with potential for arcing, insulation damage, and fire"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize circuit and re-terminate connection",
    "Torque terminal to manufacturer-specified value using calibrated torque tool",
    "Re-scan with IR after correction to confirm normal thermal profile"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 110.14 requires terminations to provide a reliable mechanical and electrical connection. An IR thermal signature indicating insufficient torque means the connection is high-resistance and heating under load, which is a fire hazard and non-compliant at rough-complete stage.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.14"]
}
